name: Envelope Sequencer
description: This algorithm is a 16-step envelope or curve sequencer. It can output a CV and/or MIDI. It shares a lot of its design with the Step Sequencer (below), and some of the documentation for this algorithm cross-references with that for the other. Each step has a curve shape, a scale, and an offset. It also has a “Division”, which can either be a repeat count (the step is repeated a number of times on subsequent clocks) or a ratchet count (the step repeats a number of times within the duration of one clock). The algorithm has a function for randomising the curves in various ways. Each Envelope Sequencer has internal storage for 32 sequences, which you can use like ‘snapshots’ or ‘patterns’ to prepare a number of sequences and then switch between them (manually, or under CV control) to build up larger structures. Remember that every curve shape, level, etc. is a parameter and therefore mappable.
short_description: A really, really big curve sequencer
gui_description: The display shows an overview of the 16 steps. The current step being played is indicated by a highlight above the step. The start and end steps are indicated by solid bars. The step whose parameter is being edited is shown highlighted. Divisions (repeats or ratchets) are shown as breaks in the highlight, with a number above to indicate the division.

parameters.name: Sequence
parameters.description: Selects the current sequence.

parameters.name: Start
parameters.description: The first step to play. A reset will jump to this step.

parameters.name: End
parameters.description: The last step to play.

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.description: The sequencer direction. See below.

parameters.name: Permutation
parameters.description: The sequencer permutation. See below.

parameters.name: Reset offset
parameters.description: Offsets the step that will jumped to when the sequencer is reset.

parameters.name: Length type
parameters.description: Sets how the length of a step is determined: “% of clock” or “Fixed”.

parameters.name: % length
parameters.description: Sets the length of the step if the type is “% of clock”.

parameters.name: Fixed length
parameters.description: Sets the length of the step if the type is “Fixed”.

parameters.name: Curve
parameters.description: Chooses the curve shape for the step.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.description: Sets the amplitude of the curve over the step.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Sets the offset added to the curve during the step.

parameters.name: Division
parameters.description: Sets the step repeat or ratchet count.

parameters.name: Mute
parameters.description: Sets the probability that the step will be muted.

parameters.name: Skip
parameters.description: Sets the probability that the step will be skipped.

parameters.name: Reset
parameters.description: Sets the probability that the step causes a reset.

parameters.name: Repeat
parameters.description: Sets the probability that the step will be repeated.

parameters.name: Randomise
parameters.description: When this parameter changes from 0 to 1, the sequence steps between the current start and end steps will be randomised according to the parameters that follow.

parameters.name: Levels
parameters.description: If set, the curve levels (scales) will be randomised.

parameters.name: Curves
parameters.description: If set, the curve shapes will be randomised.

parameters.name: Repeats
parameters.description: If set, the repeats/ratchets will be randomised.

parameters.name: Min repeat
parameters.description: The minimum repeat count, if a step is a repeat.

parameters.name: Max repeat
parameters.description: The maximum repeat count, if a step is a repeat.

parameters.name: Min ratchet
parameters.description: The minimum ratchet count, if a step is a ratchet.

parameters.name: Max ratchet
parameters.description: The maximum ratchet count, if a step is a ratchet.

parameters.name: Repeat probability
parameters.description: The probability that a step will be a repeat.

parameters.name: Ratchet probability
parameters.description: The probability that a step will be a ratchet.

parameters.name: Reset mode
parameters.description: Sets the mode for the reset input. The options are: the input is a reset trigger, the input is a run/stop signal, the input is a ‘one shot’ trigger.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

parameters.name: Sequence change mode
parameters.description: How the sequence CV will be used to change sequences: ‘Switch’ or ‘Load’. See below.

parameters.name: Follow MIDI clock
parameters.description: Sets whether the sequencer follows MIDI clock.

parameters.name: Divisor
parameters.description: Sets the clock divisor when following MIDI clock.

parameters.name: MIDI channel
parameters.description: Sets the output MIDI channel.

parameters.name: MIDI CC
parameters.description: Sets the MIDI CC number to send.

parameters.name: Output to breakout
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the breakout.

parameters.name: Output to Select Bus
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the Select Bus.

parameters.name: Output to USB
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to USB.

parameters.name: Output to internal
parameters.description: Enables internal MIDI output – that is, MIDI is sent to the other algorithms.

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: The bus to use as the clock input.

input_ports.name: Reset input
input_ports.description: The bus to use as the reset input.

input_ports.name: Sequence CV input
input_ports.description: The bus to use to select the sequence.

input_ports.name: Sequence trigger input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the sequence select trigger.

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: The bus to use as the clock input.

input_ports.name: Reset input
input_ports.description: The bus to use as the reset input.

input_ports.name: Sequence CV input
input_ports.description: The bus to use to select the sequence. See below.

input_ports.name: Sequence trigger input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the sequence select trigger. See below.

output_ports.name: CV output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the CV output.

output_ports.name: MIDI output
output_ports.description: MIDI output to breakout, Select Bus, USB, or internal algorithms.

output_ports.name: Output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the CV output.
